DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF THOMAS MARSONER, ANNABELLE DARAUYA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION ATTENDANCE AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS BY FCA US LLCS DEALERSHIP PERSONNEL MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF 2,422.50.THE COURT ADOPTS IT'S TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE FOLLOWING MODIFICATIONS: BY MAY 31, THE PARTIES WILL SUBMIT A SCHEDULE FOR THE DEPOSITIONS OF THE DEALERSHIP PERSONNEL TO GO FORWARD WITHIN TWENTY DAYS, AT THE LOCATION ONE MILE FROM THE DEALERSHIP, WHICH PLAINTIFF CHOSE. PLAINTIFF WILL NOT REQUIRE DEALERSHIP PERSONNEL TO COME TO THAT LOCATION UNTIL THEIR DEPOSTION COMMENCES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED AS EACH SIDE ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. Plaintiff Thomas Marsoner, Annabelle Darauya'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hadi Gerami, Esq., Knight Law Group, Telephone 310-552-2250, appearing for Plaintiff(s);
Jeffrey C. Schmid, Esq., Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, Telephone, 619-233-1006, appearing for Defendant FCA US, LLC; (CourtCall)

Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Thomas Marsoner, Annabelle Darauya's Motion For Attorney's Fees, Costs And Expenses.

Plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $16,963.12. In addition, Plaintiffs' unopposed motion for reimbursement of costs and expenses is granted in the amount of $6,067.23. The Court finds the amount of time expended on the case by plaintiffs' counsel and the monetary charge for that time to be excessive and unreasonable in light of the modest amount in controversy and the relative simplicity of the issues, particularly given counsel's expertise in the area and the number of attorneys (at least ten) who billed time on the case. Further, although defendants' accepted section 998 offer contemplated that the parties would attempt to stipulate to a reasonable award of fees and costs, plaintiffs made no effort before filing their motion to meet and confer. As a result, the time spent in connection with the motion (which could have been avoided altogether, had the parties stipulated to an award) was not reasonably incurred. For these reasons, the Court has subtracted that time (totaling $6,020) from the lodestar, and has reduced the remaining amount by 25%. (See Save Our Uniquely Rural Community Environment v. County of San Bernadino (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1188 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in reducing lodestar by 80 percent].) The Court declines to award a positive multiplier. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier to same counsel in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors].) Counsel for plaintiff is to submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Off Calendar May-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
George Aguilar, Esq, 310-552-2250 of Knight Law Group for Plaintiff THOMAS MARSONER, ANNABELLE DARAUYA through Court Call.
Rene Gamboa, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois for Defendant FCA US.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF THOMAS MARSONER, ANNABELLE DARAUYA's Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Fca Us Llcs Dealership Personnel Memorandum In Support Of Motion And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of 2,422.50.

The Court adopts it's tentative ruling with the following modifications: By May 31, the parties will submit a schedule for the depositions of the dealership personnel to go forward within twenty days, at the location one mile from the dealership, which Plaintiff chose. Plaintiff will not require dealership personnel to come to that location until their depostion commences. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied as each side acted with substantial justification. Plaintff to prepare a proposed form of order to comply with CRC 3.1312 and submit the proposed order directly to the Judge Pro Tem.
